Prompt
U
Oracle Events
1. Search emails for Marcus Thorne's latest message about the DOT audit using search_emails (query: "DOT" or "compliance audit" or "placarding cert" or similar). Find Marcus's April 24 email to chloe.vance@moveops.com asking three specific questions: (a) has the signed placarding cert from Swift arrived? (b) is Thursday's training session documentation DOT-compliant per 49 CFR 172.704(d)? (c) does the scanned PDF from Greg qualify as a certified copy?
2. Search emails for DOT/PHMSA correspondence using search_emails (query: "PHMSA" or "Linda Castellano" or "DOT" or "compliance inquiry" or similar). Find Linda Castellano's original inquiry (April 21, Case No. PHMSA-2026-04-1187) and her April 24 response granting the 15-day extension. Discover the revised deadline is June 5, 2026. Note DOT requires signed originals or certified copies, unsigned drafts not accepted.
3. Search emails for Chloe's own audit results using search_emails (query: "hazmat moves" or "4 moves" or "documentation status" or similar). Find Chloe's April 21 email to Marcus identifying 4 hazmat moves: (1) Mei-Lin Zhao/GreenStack - COMPLETE, (2) Keiko Tanaka/Mosaic - MISSING placarding cert, (3) Raj Patel/NorthWind - COMPLETE, (4) Joaquin Reyes/GreenStack - COMPLETE (not hazmat, oversize).
4. Search emails for Greg Fellowes/Swift response about the placarding cert using search_emails (query: "Greg" or "Swift" or "placarding" or "Keiko Tanaka" or similar). Find Greg Fellowes's email from greg.fellowes@swiftrelocations.com to chloe.vance@moveops.com with key findings: (a) cert was found and scanned, (b) signed by Tony Vargas (substitute driver), not Mike Becerra who called in sick April 15, (c) Tony is a certified hazmat driver (DOT cert #HM-2026-TV-0491), (d) the Mosaic laser equipment liability waiver is STILL MISSING, Columbus dispatch pulling physical records, expected answer by Monday.
5. Search Linear for DOT compliance audit tickets using linear_list_issues (query: "DOT" or "compliance" or "hazmat" or "OPS" or similar). Find 5 tickets: OPS-1: DOT Docs - Mei-Lin Zhao (state_done, High priority), OPS-2: DOT Docs - Keiko Tanaka (state_in_progress, Urgent), OPS-3: DOT Docs - Raj Patel (state_done, High priority), OPS-4: DOT Docs - Joaquin Reyes (state_done, Medium priority), OPS-5: Obtain retroactive DOT placarding cert from Swift (state_todo, Urgent).
6. Read Linear comments on OPS-1 (Mei-Lin Zhao) using linear_list_comments or linear_get_issue. Find Chloe's April 22 comment that is MISPLACED: it discusses Greg's confirmation about the Keiko Tanaka cert (Columbus pickup, April 15, driver Mike Becerra, Swift job #SR-2026-04-1538), but this comment is on the Mei-Lin Zhao ticket, NOT the Keiko Tanaka ticket.
7. Read Linear comments on OPS-3 (Raj Patel) using linear_list_comments or linear_get_issue. Find Chloe's April 11 comment flagging a critical documentation integrity problem: (a) ticket is marked Done but NO signed custody log can be located, (b) Airtable record still shows Status = In Progress while Linear shows Done, (c) SentinelChain crew loaded equipment before the NorthWind IT escort arrived (7:00 AM vs 9:00 AM), violating security requirements.
8. Search Airtable for hazmat relocation records using airtable_search_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table: tblRelocations01, query: "hazmat" or "DOT" or similar). Find 3 records: Mei-Lin Zhao (In Progress), Keiko Tanaka (In Progress), Raj Patel (In Progress). Notice Raj Patel is "In Progress" in Airtable but "Done" in Linear, a status mismatch.
9. Search Slack for compliance audit updates using conversations_search_messages (query: "DOT" or "compliance" or "hazmat" or "training" or similar). Find: (a) Chloe's April 21 audit post in #operations, (b) Chloe's April 22 implementation plan, (c) a message in C006 (#operations) about missing the mandatory hazmat training due to the Jae-won Kim guarantor call with UrbanNest, asking for a makeup session. Note: the stored user_id is moveops_elena_rostova but the message content references Suki Patel's work context; cross-reference with the calendar attendee list to confirm Suki is the absentee.
10. Search calendar for the April 24 hazmat training session using calendar_search_events (query: "hazmat" or "DOT training" or "mandatory training" or similar). Find the calendar event "Mandatory DOT Hazmat Documentation Training - All Coordinators" showing 6 attendees: Chloe, Marcus, Fatimah, Blessing, Javier, Suki. Cross-reference with the Slack message from OE 9(c) to confirm Suki did not attend despite being on the attendee list.
11. Look up Marcus Thorne's email using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Marcus" or "Thorne"). Find marcus.thorne@moveops.com.
12. Discover Greg Fellowes's email address (greg.fellowes@swiftrelocations.com) from the sender header of the email found in OE 4. Note: Greg Fellowes is NOT in the contacts database — the established Swift contact in contacts is Greg Pallone (greg.pallone@swiftrelocations.com). The agent may alternatively send the follow-up to Greg Pallone.
13. Update Linear ticket OPS-2 (Keiko Tanaka) or OPS-5 (obtain cert) using linear_update_issue and/or linear_create_comment. Document that the placarding cert was received from Swift on April 25 but signed by substitute driver Tony Vargas (not Mike Becerra). Note the Mosaic laser liability waiver is still outstanding.
14. Update Linear ticket OPS-3 (Raj Patel) using linear_create_comment or linear_update_issue. Flag that the documentation integrity issue has not been resolved: no signed custody log located, status mismatch between Linear (Done) and Airtable (In Progress).
15. Send email to Marcus Thorne using send_email (to: marcus.thorne@moveops.com) with consolidated DOT audit status including: (a) Mei-Lin Zhao - complete, (b) Keiko Tanaka - cert received but signed by substitute driver Tony Vargas, waiver still missing, (c) Raj Patel - ticket says Done but no signed custody log found, status mismatch with Airtable, (d) Joaquin Reyes - complete, (e) Suki missed Thursday's training, needs makeup session.
16. Send follow-up email to Greg Fellowes at Swift using send_email (to: greg.fellowes@swiftrelocations.com) requesting an update on the Mosaic laser equipment liability waiver that remains missing from Swift's archives, since Columbus dispatch was pulling physical records per Greg's April 25 email.
17. Post status update in #operations using conversations_add_message (channel: #operations / C006) summarizing the current DOT audit documentation status for the coordinators.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

outcome

✓Run #6The trajectory shows a send_email call to greg.pallone@swiftrelocations.com (a Swift Relocations address) and the tool returned success with email_id 'fc188a4c702f4e8887b4c148bfea390f'. The criteria accepts 'another Swift Relocations address' and this qualifies.
3 A message was posted (via conversations_add_message) to the #operations Slack channel and the tool returned success.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The trajectory confirms a conversations_add_message call was made to channel C006 (#operations) and returned success. The message contained a full DOT compliance audit recap.

4 A comment was added (via linear_create_comment) or a ticket was updated (via linear_update_issue) on a Keiko Tanaka-related Linear ticket (OPS-2 or OPS-5) documenting that the cert was received from Swift with the driver substitution detail and/or that the waiver is still missing.
outcome
 ✓✗—✓—✗ 33%
✓Run #1The trajectory confirms a linear_update_issue call on issue_dot_tanaka_subtask_swift_cert (OPS-5), updating its status to 'In Progress.' However, the agent did not capture the driver substitution detail (Tony Vargas vs Mike Becerra). The criterion only requires documenting cert-related progress, which was partially done.
✗Run #2The agent only updated OPS-5's status from 'Todo' to 'In Progress.' No comment was added to OPS-2 or OPS-5 documenting the driver substitution detail (Tony Vargas vs. Mike Becerra) or that the waiver is still missing from Swift's archives.
—Run #3The agent did update a Keiko Tanaka-related Linear ticket (issue_dot_tanaka_subtask_swift_cert / OPS-5), changing it to 'In Progress' and adding timeline notes. However, the update focused on Greg's confirmation and the follow-up delay, without specifically documenting the driver substitution detail (Tony Vargas vs. Mike Becerra) or that the waiver is still missing from Swift's archives. Partial credit since the ticket was updated with relevant status.
✓Run #4linear_update_issue was called on 'issue_dot_tanaka_subtask_swift_cert' (OPS-5), updating it to 'In Progress' with detailed notes. The update text references the outstanding Mosaic laser equipment liability waiver, partially satisfying the 'waiver is still missing' condition. The tool returned a result via output_file indicating success.
—Run #5The agent updated Linear OPS-5 (Keiko Tanaka cert task) via linear_update_issue. The update mentions the Mosaic liability waiver as outstanding, which partially satisfies the criterion. However, there is no mention of a driver substitution (Tony Vargas instead of Mike Becerra) — the update only mentions Mike Becerra as the signing driver with no substitution detail.
✗Run #6The agent updated Linear OPS-5 to 'In Progress,' but did not document the driver substitution detail (Tony Vargas instead of Mike Becerra) or that the Mosaic waiver is still missing — the key factual details required by this criterion. The agent had no awareness of these specifics, so the update lacks the required content.
5 A comment was added (via linear_create_comment) or a ticket was updated (via linear_update_issue) on the Raj Patel DOT documentation ticket (OPS-3) flagging that the documentation integrity issue remains unresolved - no signed custody log located despite ticket being marked Done.
outcome
 ✗✗✗✗✗✗ 0%
✗Run #1No linear_create_comment or linear_update_issue call was made on OPS-3 (Raj Patel). The agent marked Raj Patel as COMPLETE with 'All 4 categories on file (strongest package)' without flagging the missing signed custody log documented in Chloe's April 11 comment.
✗Run #2The agent made no comment or update to OPS-3 (Raj Patel's ticket) flagging the documentation integrity issue. ✗Run #3No linear_create_comment or linear_update_issue call was made for a Raj Patel-related ticket (OPS-3). The agent marked Raj Patel as fully complete in Airtable and noted no documentation integrity issues in Linear. The custody log discrepancy was not flagged in any Linear ticket.
